title: Can You Guess The Actor From Their Breakout Role Alone? (Round 2)
dek: No name, no photo until you reveal it — just the part that made them a star.

items.quote: I played a gold-digging wife opposite Leonardo DiCaprio in a three-hour fever dream about Wall Street excess — and it turned me from an unknown Australian soap actress into a movie star almost overnight.
I played a 17-year-old falling for an older graduate student over one Italian summer, in a film best remembered for a monologue about peaches — it earned me an Oscar nomination at age 22.
I spent an entire film crying, screaming, and eventually smiling while flower-crowned strangers decided my fate at a Swedish midsummer festival that goes very, very wrong.
I played a real young man from Oakland on the last day of his life, based on a true story that sparked national outrage over how he died.
In my first-ever leading role, I lived alone with my Puritan family on the edge of the New England wilderness in 1630, slowly becoming everyone's prime suspect for witchcraft.
I spent well over a year training my voice and body to play one of the most famous musicians who ever lived — and it took me months afterward to fully sound like myself again.
